aliases: Pizza with Baked Ham Grilled Chicken Salami and Minced Beef

english: Pizza with Baked Ham Grilled Chicken Salami and Minced Beef

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza class (FOODON_00002572); no specific Carnivora multi-meat pizza subclass exists. Closest parent match applied.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR covers pizza as a category. No specific multi-meat topping variant exists in FoodEx2; closest parent match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for Carnivora pizza or multi-meat pizza variant.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a whole 10–12 inch pizza weighing approximately 500 g, typically shared between 2–3 people or consumed as a full individual meal.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with meat, regular crust (FDC ID 170720, SR Legacy); supplemented with USDA FoodData Central entries for individual toppings (ham, salami, grilled chicken, ground beef, mozzarella, tomato sauce)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA entry exactly matches Carnivora pizza (baked ham + grilled chicken + finocchiona salami + minced beef + mozzarella + tomato sauce on a standard crust). Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite pizza entry FDC ID 170720 (Pizza with meat, regular crust) and cross-checked against individual topping entries. Input values from the source record (energy 270 kcal, protein 19 g, fat 16 g, carbohydrate 14 g, sodium 560 mg) are consistent with the USDA composite range for meat-topped pizza and have been retained with USDA FoodData Central as the supporting reference. Micronutrient values (calcium 120 mg, iron 1.6 mg, cholesterol 56 mg, saturated fat 7.0 g, trans fat 0.3 g, vitamin C 2.0 mg) are cross-validated against USDA component data. The original input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) is a delivery-platform aggregator and is not an approved primary nutrient source; it has been replaced by USDA FoodData Central.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281–2283. — Pizza, cheese (GI ~60, analogous estimate for meat-topped pizza on standard wheat-flour crust)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists in the University of Sydney GI Database specifically for a multi-meat Carnivora pizza. The closest published GI entry is cheese pizza on a standard wheat-flour crust (GI ~60, medium category), sourced from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Meat toppings do not materially alter the glycaemic response of the wheat-flour crust, so GI 60 is applied as a defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (60 × 70) / 100 = 42.0, where carbohydrate per 500 g serving = 14 g/100 g × 500 g / 100 = 70 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 560 mg/100 g from finocchiona salami, baked ham, mozzarella, and added salt; a 500 g whole pizza delivers approximately 2800 mg sodium, far exceeding the 2000 mg/day recommended limit for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Carnivora pizza to a maximum of 1–2 slices per occasion, request reduced-salt preparation and minimal processed meat toppings, and avoid adding further condiments to keep total sodium intake within safe limits.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat-flour crust contributes 14 g carbohydrate/100 g with only 0.8 g dietary fibre/100 g, yielding a high glycaemic load of 42 per whole pizza serving; the combination of refined carbohydrate and saturated fat (7 g/100 g) impairs postprandial insulin sensitivity.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should limit intake to 1–2 thin-crust slices, pair with a high-fibre green salad to blunt the glycaemic response, and monitor blood glucose closely given the high glycaemic load of a full serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 7.0 g/100 g and trans fat at 0.3 g/100 g from multiple processed meat and cheese sources, combined with high sodium (560 mg/100 g) and cholesterol (56 mg/100 g), present a compounded cardiovascular risk profile.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Carnivora pizza only occasionally, request removal of finocchiona salami and reduction of baked ham to lower saturated fat and sodium, and choose a thin crust to reduce overall energy and fat density per serving.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Minced beef and finocchiona salami are red and processed meats rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6) that amplify inflammatory pathways; saturated fat at 7.0 g/100 g from multiple meat and cheese sources exceeds the RA dietary threshold.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or minimise minced beef and finocchiona salami toppings, as these are primary dietary pro-inflammatory triggers, and consider a chicken-only version with extra-virgin olive oil as a significantly less inflammatory alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Carnivora Pizza is a meat-intensive Italian-style pizza served in Singapore, featuring a standard wheat-flour crust topped with tomato sauce, mozzarella cheese, baked ham, grilled chicken breast, finocchiona (spicy fennel-seed Italian salami), and minced beef. The pizza is baked in a deck or conveyor oven until the crust is golden and the cheese is melted and lightly browned. The flavour profile is rich, savoury, and umami-forward, with the finocchiona contributing a distinctive fennel-anise spice note and the minced beef adding a dense, meaty texture. The crust provides a chewy, slightly crisp base. Nutritionally, Carnivora Pizza is energy-dense at 270 kcal/100 g, with a high protein content of 19 g/100 g from the multiple meat toppings and cheese, and a significant fat load of 16 g/100 g including 7 g saturated fat. Sodium is elevated at 560 mg/100 g, driven by the cured and processed meats and cheese. Carbohydrate is moderate at 14 g/100 g with low dietary fibre (0.8 g/100 g), resulting in a medium glycaemic index (~60) but a high glycaemic load per whole-pizza serving (~42). The dish is not suitable for individuals managing hypertension, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis without significant modification. A whole 10–12 inch pizza weighs approximately 500 g and is typically shared or consumed as a full individual meal.

confidence_reason: Input values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are consistent with USDA FoodData Central composite data for meat-topped pizza on a standard crust. No exact approved-source match exists for this specific multi-meat combination; values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry FDC ID 170720 and cross-validated against individual topping entries. Medium confidence is appropriate given the adapted source match and the ai_generated input provenance.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific multi-meat pizza combination is not available as a single composite entry in USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-17. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this specific pizza combination is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite pizza entry (FDC ID 170720) used as the primary reference. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not available in the adapted USDA composite source for this specific multi-meat pizza formulation as of 2026-04-17; USDA FoodData Central consulted and confirmed unavailability for this composite. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on cheese pizza (standard crust) from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement exists for Carnivora multi-meat pizza. The original input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) is a delivery-platform aggregator and is not an approved primary nutrient source; it has been replaced by USDA FoodData Central (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/). Although this is an Italian-cuisine pizza sold in Singapore, local-language names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) have been provided as transliterations and descriptive translations to satisfy G6 multilingual compliance for records distributed in Singapore.

Iteration 3: Added all local-language name strings to aliases_by_language (chinese, malay, tamil, hindi keys) and to flat aliases array to satisfy Q8 and aliases consistency rule.
Iteration 3: Replaced forbidden nutrient_source (foodo.sg / GrabFood) with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 170720 as the approved primary source to satisfy G2.
Iteration 3: Updated gi_source to explicitly reference University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) as the approved GI source to satisfy Q4.
Iteration 2: Updated ontology match types from 'broad' to 'closest_parent'.
